summary: In patients with tongue thrusting habit, you would observe signs like spaced or crowded front teeth; however, a properly aligned first molar contact point at rest will remain intact as this condition doesn't influence such occlusal relationships specifically related to anterior tooth position. To clarify further, while other options may be affected indirectly through changes in jaw positions influenced by continuous lingual pressure during swallowing, an isolated flush terminal plane relationship should persist unless otherwise impacted independently from these habitual pressures. Agent2 reasoned based primarily on known dental impacts associated with tongue thrusting—such as gaps, overcrowded areas, and misalignments—and excluded those unrelated aspects affecting only specific types of malocclusions where no direct interference occurs given typical muscular actions involved in tongue-thrust behaviors. Therefore, option D representing "flush terminal plane" was identified correctly among provided choices for its resistance against alteration under normal circumstances described here concerning
